items.title: Somalia: Turkey Renews Somalia Military Deployment for Two More Years
items.description: [Radio Dalsan] Turkey's parliament has approved a two-year extension of its military mission in Somalia, a move set to take effect on July 27 that reaffirms Ankara's continued backing of Somali security institutions. The decision allows Turkish forces to keep providing training, advisory support and technical assistance to Somalia's National Army.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Turkey Renews Somalia Military Deployment for Two More Years

items.title: Ambassador to Senegal Opens Goreé School for Sustainable Migration
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 21 LUG - Italian Ambassador to Senegal Michele Tommasi attended the opening ceremony of the Gorée School 2026, organized as part of the project “Training, Dignity, Inclusion, and Innovation in Senegal,” funded by Italian Cooperation. Organized by the Institute for the Rights of Migrant Workers, in partnership with Inca Cgil Senegal, VIS, and numerous Italian and Senegalese institutional and social actors, the Gorée School serves as a unique forum for dialogue and exchange. It brings together institutions, labor unions, development cooperation actors, civil society organizati
items.titleIt: Ambassador to Senegal Opens Goreé School for Sustainable Migration

items.title: An expert from the Guardia di Finanza at the Italian Embassy in Riyadh
items.description: (ANSA) - IL CAIRO, 19 LUG - The Italian Embassy in Riyadh has strengthened its structure with the establishment of a Guardia di Finanza Office, headed by Colonel Alessandro Coscarelli in his capacity as an expert from the Corps. The opening of the new office represents an important step in consolidating the partnership between Italy and Saudi Arabia, with the goal of strengthening cooperation in the field of economic and financial security. It joins the other 29 Financial Police Expert offices currently part of the Corps’ overseas network at bilateral and multilateral diplomatic missions. The
items.titleIt: An expert from the Guardia di Finanza at the Italian Embassy in Riyadh

items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Kenya: Zoho Champions Digital Transformation of Kenyan SMEs Through a Strategic Partnership With KNCCI
items.description: [Capital FM] Zoho, a global technology company, today announced a strategic partnership in Kenya aimed at accelerating SME growth, advancing digital transformation, and supporting entrepreneurs across the country. The announcement was made during Zoholics Kenya 2026, Zoho's annual user conference.
items.titleIt: Kenya: Zoho Champions Digital Transformation of Kenyan SMEs Through a Strategic Partnership With KNCCI

items.title: FIGC reportedly approached Guardiola for Italy job
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 20 - Italian Soccer Federation (FIGC) technical director Paolo Maldini and consultant Leonardo have been spotted in Barcelona, amid reports by Sky Sport that they met Pep Guardiola at the weekend to talk to him about the vacant position of Italy coach. The former Barcelona, Bayern Munich and Manchester City boss is currently taking a break from soccer after 10 years in the Premier League. Other options include Andrea Pirlo and Roberto Mancini. 2006 World Cup hero Pirlo is the manager of UAE First Division League club Dubai United, while former Italy coach Mancini is currentl
items.titleIt: FIGC reportedly approached Guardiola for Italy job

items.title: Rome to host meeting for Two-State solution July 28-29 - Tajani
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 22 - Rome will host a meeting of the Global Alliance for a Two-State Solution to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, Deputy Premier and Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said on Wednesday as he reported to parliament on the recent NATO summit. "On July 28 and 29 I will chair a meeting in Rome of the Global Alliance for the Two-State Solution together with the Saudi Foreign Minister," Tajani told a joint hearing of the foreign affairs and defence committees of the Senate and the Lower House. items.title: Sudan: Hunger, Disease, Dispair - Darfur's Displaced Bear Ever-Mounting Toll of Sudan War
items.description: [Dabanga] Darfur / Amsterdam -- The General Coordination of Camps for the Displaced and Refugees in Darfur warns of a catastrophic deterioration in humanitarian conditions across displacement camps in Sudan's Darfur region. Yagoub Furi, the coordination's general coordinator, tells Radio Dabanga that camps in North Darfur - with the exception of Tawila camp - are experiencing a complete absence of food aid, particularly the camps at Fata Borno, Kutum and Kassab.
items.titleIt: Sudan: Hunger, Disease, Dispair - Darfur's Displaced Bear Ever-Mounting Toll of Sudan War
